qa_pairs.question: What is the CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCDB is the portal to your account with the Alliance, providing information on your project roles, allocated resources, and resource usage statistics.

qa_pairs.question: What tasks can a user perform on the CCDB portal?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can register, manage personal information and roles, apply for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), and manage RAP information and membership.

qa_pairs.question: What is a CCI and why would I want one?
qa_pairs.answer: A CCI is a unique personal and national identifier created when you register at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca. It's a string like 'abc-123'.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to get a CCI?
qa_pairs.answer: You must be a faculty member at a Canadian institution registered with the CCDB, or be sponsored by such a faculty member. This includes graduate students and research staff.

qa_pairs.question: What is a 'role' in the Alliance system?
qa_pairs.answer: A role is an identifier representing a person's position (e.g., faculty, graduate student), institution, and sponsor. A valid and up-to-date role is necessary to access computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: What is a CCRI and how is it formatted?
qa_pairs.answer: A CCRI is the unique identifier for your role, formatted as CCI-2 digits (e.g., abc-123-01). One CCI can be associated with multiple CCRIs over time, as each job on a national cluster is attributed to a CCRI.

qa_pairs.question: What is a Resource Allocation Project (RAP)?
qa_pairs.answer: RAPs are how PIs access national computational resources. They are identified by a RAPI and an associated group name, and resources are allocated to groups of researchers, not individuals.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main types of RAPs?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main types are the Default RAP, automatically created when a PI role is activated for opportunistic use with lowest priority, and the RAC RAP, created when a PI receives an award through the RAC application process.

qa_pairs.question: What is a group name in the context of RAPs?
qa_pairs.answer: A group name is an alias for the Research Allocation Project Identifier (RAPI). Each RAPI has a unique group name, which is often easier for users to remember and is used as a POSIX group name.

qa_pairs.question: Who has access to a Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: All of a PI's activated sponsored user roles are automatically members of the PI's Default RAP, a membership that cannot be modified, though a PI can deactivate any sponsored role.

qa_pairs.question: How does membership for a RAC RAP with HPC resources work?
qa_pairs.answer: When an HPC RAC RAP is created, all of the PI's sponsored user roles, all associated Co-PI roles, and all sponsored user roles of the Co-PIs are automatically added as members. New sponsored roles for the PI are also added automatically.

qa_pairs.question: How does membership for a RAC RAP with Cloud resources work?
qa_pairs.answer: For RAC RAPs with Cloud resources allocated, only the PI is added as a member at the time the RAP is created.

qa_pairs.question: What are the three permission levels for RAC RAP membership?
qa_pairs.answer: The three permission levels are Owner (the PI, who manages everything), Manager (delegated permission to change membership and use allocations on CCDB only), and Member (can use allocations but cannot modify membership).

qa_pairs.question: If I had an Alliance account but my position or sponsor changed, or I lost my password, should I create a new one?
qa_pairs.answer: No, each person is allowed only one Alliance account (CCI). If your position or sponsor changes, apply for a new role. If you forgot your password, reset it. If you can't access your email, contact accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: How do I sign up for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Go to the register link on the CCDB portal. You will need to accept certain policies and agreements to create an account.

qa_pairs.question: What types of roles can I select when applying for an account?
qa_pairs.answer: You can select sponsor roles (for faculty members or PIs) or sponsored roles, which include students, postdoctoral fellows, researchers affiliated with the same institution as the PI, non-research staff, and external collaborators.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after I submit my Alliance account request?
qa_pairs.answer: You will receive an email to confirm your address. If you are a PI, a Federation staff will approve your application. If you are a sponsored user, your sponsor must confirm your application before Federation staff approval.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I get help for questions not covered in this FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: For any questions not covered, you can send an email to accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.
